assumptions.name: Model start
assumptions.source: [BP date 2026-04-25] assume operations forecast begins the following month.

assumptions.name: Starting cash / pre-seed proceeds at M1
assumptions.source: [BP ask $2–4M pre-seed] modeled at midpoint-high end to include required 6-month buffer.

assumptions.name: List price per managed endpoint
assumptions.source: [Research market SOM] 1,250 devices × $160 annual software value per device.

assumptions.name: Average endpoints in paid pilot
assumptions.source: [BP MVP/pilot scope] narrow first workflow heuristic for regulated deployments.

assumptions.name: Average endpoints in initial production deployment
assumptions.source: [Research SOM 1,250 devices/account] conservative step before full fleet rollout.

assumptions.name: Average endpoints in mature production account
assumptions.source: [BP market SOM and research bottom-up] reachable account size estimate.

assumptions.name: Pilot recurring revenue
assumptions.unit: K USD per month per account

assumptions.name: Initial production recurring revenue
assumptions.unit: K USD per month per account
assumptions.source: [A3×A5 calc] 900 × $160 / 12 = $12.0K MRR before overages.

assumptions.name: Mature production recurring revenue
assumptions.unit: K USD per month per account
assumptions.source: [A3×A6 calc] 1,250 × $160 / 12 ≈ $16.7K MRR before overages.

assumptions.name: Target gross margin
assumptions.source: [BP businessModel.targetGrossMarginPct]

assumptions.name: First paid pilot timing
assumptions.source: [Research procurement friction + BP goal of 3 paid pilots in 12 months] conservative long-cycle enterprise ramp.

assumptions.name: Year 1 paid-customer ramp
assumptions.source: [BP milestone] secure 3 paid pilots inside first 12 months.

assumptions.name: Year 2 end-of-quarter customer counts
assumptions.source: [BP 12–24 month milestone] 2 pilot-to-production conversions plus modest new-logo additions.

assumptions.name: Year 3 end-of-quarter customer counts
assumptions.source: [Research SOM 30 reachable accounts] model captures 13 by Q4Y3, below SOM.

assumptions.name: Blended monthly revenue per active account by quarter
assumptions.source: [A7-A9 mix-shift calc] pilots convert into production and expand toward mature endpoint counts.

assumptions.name: Hiring plan
assumptions.value: 2 founders at start; founding engineer at start; product lead by Q2Y1; security engineer by Q3Y1; partnerships/AE by Q4Y1; then paced engineering, CS, sales, and product hires through Y3
assumptions.source: [BP team section] plus startup-finance heuristic to support pilots then production accounts.

assumptions.name: Loaded salary bands
assumptions.value: Leadership 341; Engineering 176; Product 154; Security 182; Sales/Partnerships 176; CS/Implementation 132
assumptions.unit: K USD annualized per FTE or team where noted
assumptions.source: Startup cash-comp heuristic for US seed-stage infra startups, including ~10% payroll tax/benefits.

assumptions.name: Non-payroll opex posture
assumptions.value: Lean cloud, audit, travel, and legal spend scaled beneath payroll; no large paid marketing engine before product proof
assumptions.source: [Research partner-led GTM + long enterprise cycles] conservative spend discipline.

assumptions.name: CAC
assumptions.source: Enterprise infra heuristic: founder-led plus partner-assisted 9-month cycle with 1–2 FTE-equivalent selling effort.

assumptions.name: Monthly churn
assumptions.source: Conservative early-stage enterprise software heuristic; used for unit economics, not explicit customer-count reductions because losses round to <1 account in the modeled base case.

assumptions.name: Cash flow simplification
assumptions.source: Assume negligible debt, capex, and working-capital distortion at this stage.

fundingAsk.milestone: Reach 2+ pilot-to-production conversions, one OEM/SI channel, >$1M exit ARR, and seed-ready proof that compliant edge AI fleets can scale across accounts.

downside.description: Pilots slip by one quarter, only one pilot converts by mid-Y2, and blended account size stays closer to initial deployments.
keyAssumptionChanges: First paid pilot moves from M6 to M9
Y3 Q4 customers end at 9 instead of 13
Blended gross margin falls from 75% to 72%

base.description: Three paid pilots in Y1, two convert to production in Y2, and partner-assisted enterprise sales steadily expand account size.
keyAssumptionChanges: Customer count path ends at 13 by Q4Y3
Gross margin stays at BP target 75%
Hiring follows BP roles plus paced follow-on hires

upside.description: One vertical converts faster, OEM channel contributes earlier, and production accounts expand toward full-fleet rollout sooner.
keyAssumptionChanges: Q4Y3 customers end at 16 instead of 13
Blended account MRR reaches $22K by Q4Y3
Gross margin improves to 77% as connector reuse rises

modelDiagram.title: unit economics flow
modelDiagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Leads --> PaidPilots
  PaidPilots --> ProductionAccounts
  ProductionAccounts --> ManagedEndpoints
  ManagedEndpoints --> Revenue
  Revenue --> GrossProfit
  GrossProfit --> EBITDA
  EBITDA --> Cash

modelSanity.label: Revenue engine
modelSanity.value: Three paid pilots in Y1, two convert to production in Y2, and partner-assisted enterprise sales steadily expand account size.

modelSanity.label: Must go right
modelSanity.value: Reach 13 paying accounts by Q4Y3 while keeping gross margin near 75%.

modelSanity.label: Model breaks if
modelSanity.value: Sales cycle moves against plan (12 months); modeled cash impact is $-340K.

modelSanity.label: Next-round proof
modelSanity.value: Reach 2+ pilot-to-production conversions, one OEM/SI channel, >$1M exit ARR, and seed-ready proof that compliant edge AI fleets can scale across accounts.

sanityChecks.ruleOf40: Y3 growth 179.2% + EBITDA margin -36.7% = 142.5%; directionally strong only because revenue base is still small.
sanityChecks.burnMultiple: Y2 burn multiple about 1.2x using $1.11M EBITDA burn over roughly $0.92M net new ARR; improves in Y3 as Q4 approaches breakeven.
sanityChecks.revenuePerFte: $171K Y3 on ~11.75 average FTE (below mature SaaS benchmark $200–400K, but plausible for pre-scale enterprise infra with heavy implementation).
flags: The model stays cash-positive through Y3 only if hiring remains paced and the company lands 13 paying accounts by Q4Y3.
75% gross margin is consistent with the BP target, but real pilot gross margin could be lower if implementations become too custom.
Revenue per FTE is still light by Y3, implying the company likely needs a seed round before true cashflow breakeven unless account expansion accelerates.
Unit-economics LTV/CAC looks strong on paper; actual realized retention must be validated because the category is young and buyers have incumbent substitutes.
